kept.role: primary brand — gold/tan, dominant color in the MOBJJ logo wordmark; reads as 'belt rank / lineage / championship'
secondary brand — warm dark brown, used in the logo's deeper accent and as an alternate text color on the current site
tertiary brand — navy, used on the Heroes Jiu-Jitsu and Adopt-A-Cop pages for the affiliation crest tone; supports the military/first-responder programs without competing with the gold
text on dark — body copy color when running on the dark substrate

dropped.reason: default light-gray body background of the GoHighLevel template — not a brand color, just template chrome
duplicate near-neutral light gray from the same template; redundant
default dark text color from the template; replaced by warmed text-primary
secondary dark gray from the template footer; replaced by the warmer brand-aligned dark
true black from various CSS rules; we shift to a warm dark substrate that flatters the gold accent
Mapbox embed UI color — third-party widget bleed, not a brand color
yellow on the pricing page — appears to be a one-off CTA color from a template button, not part of the brand identity (gold #B79C54 is the real brand gold)
pale yellow tint from the affiliation page slider chrome; not brand

contrastIssues: #B79C54 on #FFFFFF = 2.92:1 — fails WCAG AA for body text (needs 4.5:1) and fails 3:1 for large text. Gold-on-white on the current site is decorative only.
#B79C54 on #0B0B0E (proposed dark substrate) = 8.4:1 — passes AAA for body. Putting the gold on dark is the right move both for accessibility and for the championship-belt energy.
Current site has zero surface elevation — every section is the same #EEEEEE light gray with no card differentiation.
colorAudit.harmonyType: warm-tertiary triad: gold + brown + navy. The gold (#B79C54) and brown (#463939) are analogous warms; the navy (#223855) sits across the wheel and acts as a complementary cooling agent for the affiliation/military pages. Clean three-color system once neutrals are stripped out.
colorAudit.hierarchyStatus: weak on the current site — the gold only appears in the logo and a few headlines, then disappears. The corrected kit promotes gold to the primary accent on every CTA + key headline.
colorAudit.saturationNotes: Original gold #B79C54 is a slightly muted antique gold (~52% saturation, 53% lightness). Premium and grown-up — perfect for the Tom DeBlass affiliate / lineage positioning. We boost it ~6% saturation and lift it ~3% in lightness for more on-screen brilliance, then keep a hover variant at the original muted tone.

colorShifts: Shifted background from template #EEEEEE (light gray) to a warm true-black #0B0A08 — gives the gold room to shine and lands the Precision-Barracks dark-mode performance-athletic register the user requested.
Lifted the brand gold from antique #B79C54 → #D4B260 (~7% more saturation, +5% brightness) — keeps the muted-belt-gold feel but reads sharper on dark mode for headlines and CTAs.
Added --accent-hot #E5C271 for kinetic-marquee glow moments and hero accents (where the brighter gold is wanted) and --accent-deep #7A6230 for stamp/badge backgrounds.
Created two-tier surface elevation (--bg-card #15130E + --bg-elev #1E1A12) — both warm-tinted to honor the gold + brown brand temperature instead of a neutral cool-black.

Warmed text-primary to #F5EFE3 (cream tinted toward the gold) instead of pure #FFFFFF — matches the lineage/heritage feel of a Tom DeBlass affiliate without going so cream that it feels like a heritage-leather kit.

Kept the navy #223855 as --tertiary (shifted to #2B4263 for slightly better dark-mode pop) — reserved exclusively for the Heroes Jiu-Jitsu, Adopt A Cop, and Tom DeBlass pages where the affiliation crest energy belongs. Never used as a primary accent.
Kept the warm brown #463939 as --secondary (shifted to #5C4A3A for elev contrast) — used for sub-divider tones and instructor-card frames so it whispers under the gold without competing.

fontRecommendation.display: Archivo Black — heavy industrial sans, all-caps, tight letter-spacing, oversized for kinetic marquees and program headers
fontRecommendation.body: Inter 400 / 500 / 700 — clean workhorse for paragraphs, nav, schedule grid, FAQ accordion
fontRecommendation.stencil: Rubik Mono One (sparingly) — for one-word callouts: TECHNIQUE. PRESSURE. PRECISION. CONNECTION. Borrowed from the Precision Barracks pattern but with copy that pulls from Mooresville's actual member reviews.
fontRecommendation.reasoning: Per user direction, the design models Precision Jiu-Jitsu Barracks. We reuse the exact same Archivo Black + Inter + Rubik Mono One stack so the visual register is identical, then differentiate by accent color (gold vs Precision's red), stencil words (TECHNIQUE/PRESSURE/PRECISION/CONNECTION vs DISCIPLINE/PRESSURE/PRECISION), and content (Tom DeBlass lineage instead of military 'Barracks' theme).

designStyle: performance-athletic-skill (Precision Barracks template — gold accent variant)

inherited: Dark-mode true-black substrate with two-tier surface elevation
Archivo Black display + Inter body + Rubik Mono One stencil typography stack
Kinetic marquee strips with oversized letter-spacing
Stencil one-word callouts in Rubik Mono One
Stat strip / oversized numbers above section headers
differentiated: Accent: brand gold #D4B260 instead of Precision's racing red #E61E2C
Substrate warmth: warm true-black #0B0A08 (gold-tinted) instead of Precision's cool true-black #0B0B0E
Stencil words: TECHNIQUE / PRESSURE / PRECISION / CONNECTION (pulled from Mooresville member reviews) instead of Precision's DISCIPLINE / PRESSURE / PRECISION (military barracks theme)
Affiliation pages: Tom DeBlass lineage + Heroes Jiu-Jitsu + Adopt A Cop (Mooresville's actual programs) instead of Precision's military Barracks identity
Color story: gold + warm brown + navy crest (heritage / lineage) instead of red + true-black (combat warning)
